scope_note: Standard 6 ran an unconditional full-scan of agents/*.md + commands/*.md per Standard 6 exception; all other standards scoped to the changed-files list above. Pre-diff baseline content was checked via `git show d6e9c12:<file>` and `git diff d6e9c12 -- <file>` to determine introduced_in_diff for each finding.

violations.finding: Bare `python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID` invocation without venv activation. Standard 3 mandates `source venv` (or `source .venv`) prefix for any python3 invocation in command .md files. The same file uses the correct `bash ~/.claude/scripts/...` form for all score-update.sh invocations (lines 415, 450-452), so this line is inconsistent with the file's own convention.
violations.recommendation: Replace with: `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id "$TASK_ID"` — or wrap in a thin bash shim and use the `bash scripts/...` pattern already established for score-update.sh in the same file.
violations.diff_evidence: Pre-diff baseline (commit d6e9c12) of commands/close.md contained zero `python3` occurrences; both lines 170 and 177 appear in the cycle diff as `+` lines (Step 0 parallel-dev shard detection section is newly added).

violations.finding: Bare `python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID` invocation without venv activation (second occurrence in the Step 0 Case 2 branch). Same rule as the line-170 finding.
violations.recommendation: Same fix as the line-170 finding — apply both call sites identically.
violations.diff_evidence: Pre-diff baseline at d6e9c12 had zero python3 occurrences in commands/close.md; this line is part of the same NEW Step 0 block added in this cycle.

standards_passed: no-inline-code (Standard 1) — pre-existing bash-as-contract pattern in agents/changelog-analyst.md applies equally to the NEW SHA-stable detection block at lines 582-600; consistent with the file's documented contract style across Phases 1-10 (see codex_consult finding #2 disposition)

no-hardcoded-values (Standard 2) — scripts/score-update.sh accepts --scores-file/--agent/--event/--note; M1 EVENT_DELTAS rebalance values (lines 84-98) are user-intentional config data per dispatch directive; RANK_BOUNDARIES and CANONICAL_AGENTS are canonical spec constants
integer-step-numbering (Standard 4) — no Step N.M or Step Na patterns in any of the 3 changed source files (Round 1b / Round 2 references in close.md are pre-existing at d6e9c12, not NEW)
meaningful-naming (Standard 5) — no forbidden patterns (enhance-*/fast-*/optimize-*/v2/improved-*) in any changed file

english-only (Standard 6) — unconditional full-scan of agents/*.md + commands/*.md (per Standard 6 exception) found zero CJK characters in any of the 3 changed source files; the single CJK hit in agents/style-inspector.md:333 is the inspector's own detection regex and is out of changed-files scope; score-update.sh comment lines 27-29 reference CJK rank labels but the comment text itself is English-only
merge-similar-scripts (Standard 7) — no new scripts created this cycle

concise-documentation (Standard 8) — added prose is contract specification (M3 <options> XML, M4 session-summary contract, M5/M6 whitelist tiers, Step 2 verdict branches, dual-final-line clarification); dev report diff_stats lines_added=212 is justified per dev report's `justification_for_overage` field
parameterized-scripts (Standard 9) — scripts/score-update.sh is fully parameterized (4 named flags + getopts-style loop)

dangling-references (Standard 10) — all 4 referenced scripts exist on disk: scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py (16886 bytes), scripts/score-update.sh (6643 bytes), scripts/cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh (328 bytes), scripts/resolve-dev-report.py (2838 bytes)
merge-conflicts (Standard 11) — repo-wide grep for ^<<<<<<<, ^=======, ^>>>>>>> markers returned zero hits in any source file

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed FINDING-1 (Standard 3 venv) as a real blocker — both close.md:170 and :177 are NEW in the diff vs baseline d6e9c12, and they are executable slash-command instructions, not passive prose. Codex flagged one additional alleged Standard-1 violation at agents/changelog-analyst.md:582-600 (NEW SHA-stable detection bash block); this is verified as NEW in the diff but evaluated as consistent with the file's pre-existing bash-as-contract pattern used throughout Phases 1-10 (e.g., the identical convention at pre-existing lines 60-65, 184-191, 200-202). One observation_only finding (audit regex for lettered substeps could be improved) — non-cycle-blocking.

findings.issue: FINDING-1 is real — commands/close.md:170 and :177 are executable instructions, not passive prose; baseline d6e9c12 had no python3 in close.md.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id "$TASK_ID"` or add a real shell wrapper.
findings.rationale: Codex's finding matches the draft; both call sites are recorded as Standard 3 violations in violations[] above with introduced_in_diff: true. Applied per codex's PROPOSED_FIX.

findings.issue: Missed NEW Standard 1 violation: agents/changelog-analyst.md:582-600 adds bash command blocks (git rev-parse, git show, grep) — not covered by M5/M6 prose exception.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract the detection into a parameterized helper script and keep only the contract/output schema in the agent doc.
findings.rationale: The pattern is NEW in this diff but is functionally identical to the file's pre-existing bash-as-contract convention used throughout Phases 1-10 (pre-existing identical-style git command blocks at lines 60-65 Phase 1, 184-191 Phase 3, 200-202 Phase 4, 232-247 Phase 6, etc.). Extracting only this newly-added 3-command detection into a separate script while leaving 7+ similar pre-existing blocks in place would create inconsistency without addressing the broader pattern. The Standard 1 rule targets logic that should live in scripts — but for an 'agentic' subagent file like changelog-analyst.md, the bash commands ARE the contract specification (the agent interprets and executes them; they are not duplicating script-extractable logic). The codex fix should be filed as a separate refactor cycle covering the whole file, not bolted on to this cycle's scope. Recording as rejected with rationale, not applied this cycle.

findings.issue: Targeted checks: no CJK chars in score-update.sh:27-29 (comments are English-only); no NEW script hardcoding in M4/M5/M6; lettered substep markers like 'Round 1b' are pre-existing at d6e9c12, not NEW. Draft Std 4 grep is too narrow but conclusion is OK for this diff.
findings.proposed_fix: No cycle fix required; improve audit regex to include lettered substeps and baseline comparison in future inspector revisions.
findings.rationale: Codex confirms three targeted negative checks (no NEW Std-6/Std-2/Std-4 violations). The audit-tooling improvement is a separate concern for the inspector spec, not a finding against this cycle's diff.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex's blocker finding #1 directly confirms and refines FINDING-1 in the draft (already in violations[]). Codex's blocker finding #2 is logged as rejected with a documented rationale (consistency with pre-existing convention; file-scope refactor is out of cycle scope). Codex's observation_only finding is logged with disposition=logged. Total violations after codex review: 2 (both Standard 3, both introduced_in_diff: true).
